--- title: "{{< var md-01.title >}}" subtitle: "{{< var course.long-title >}}" author: "Lars Schöbitz" date: "2026-06-04" date-format: long footer: "[{{< fa link >}} {{< var course.site-short >}}]({{< var course.site >}})" logo: "../images/ghe-square-logo-vector.svg" format: revealjs: theme: ../style/slides.scss transition: fade slide-number: true chalkboard: true editor: source execute: freeze: auto echo: true --- # Welcome! {{< fa hand >}} {background-color="#4C326A"} ## Meet the lecturer {.smaller} **Lars Schöbitz** ![](images/lars_schoebitz_profile_photo.jpg){fig-alt="Headshot of Lars Schöbitz" fig-align="left" width=40%} - Environmental Engineer {{< fa hammer >}} - Retired researcher {{< fa bed >}} - [RStudio certified instructor](https://education.rstudio.com/trainers/) - [Data steward at ETHZ](https://ghe.ethz.ch/ghe-blog-news/2024/02/blog-attention-prof-you-need-a-data-steward-for-your-team.html) ## Learning objectives 1. Learn to use the Quarto file format and the RStudio IDE visual editing mode to produce scholarly documents with citations, cross-references, figures, and tables. 2. Learn to use Quarto Pub to publish a website and share research with a broader audience. ## Your turn ::: task Think about the last time you published a written document: - Which tasks gave you joy? - Which tasks were challenging or frustrating? ::: hand Take some written notes. ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: # Quarto {.center-align background-color="#4C326A"} ::: hand authoring and publishing workflows for collaborative scientific writing ::: ## {{< fa bullseye >}} {.center-align auto-animate="true"} ::: {style="margin-top: 50px; font-size: 1.5em"} Every student will have a personal, published, free [website]{.highlight-yellow} that links to their (academic) [profiles]{.highlight-yellow} and has the potential to be used as a (scientific) [blog]{.highlight-yellow}. ::: ## {{< fa toolbox >}} {.center-align auto-animate="true"} ::: {style="margin-top: 50px; font-size: 1.5em"} Quarto\ RStudio IDE on Posit Cloud\ Quarto Pub ::: # Why? -\> Reproduciblity {.center-align background-color="#4C326A"} ## Why reproducibility ::: incremental - Publisher data and code policies are tightening. - Reproducible documents reduce the "copy-paste" gap between analysis and write-up. - One source file, many outputs: HTML, PDF, Word, slides. ::: ::: aside Full publisher-policy walkthrough: see [overview slides](../0-1-overview/index.qmd) ::: ## Course structure - [My turn]{.highlight-yellow}: Lecture segments + live coding - [Our turn]{.highlight-yellow}: Live coding + follow along - [Your turn]{.highlight-yellow}: Exercises in break-out rooms # Hello Quarto {background-color="#4C326A"} ## Meeting you where you are ::: columns ::: {.column width="50%"} ::: {.fragment .fade-in-then-semi-out} I'll **assume** you - do [not]{.highlight-yellow} have R experience - have [not]{.highlight-yellow} worked in an IDE before (e.g. RStudio IDE) - want to learn about Quarto ::: ::: ::: {.column width="50%"} ::: {.fragment .fade-in} I'll **teach** you - Quarto syntax and formats - Markdown - Some R along the way! ::: ::: ::: # What is Quarto? {background-color="#4C326A"} ## Quarto ... - is a new, open-source, scientific, and technical publishing system - aims to make the process of creating and collaborating dramatically better ![Artwork from "Hello, Quarto" keynote by Julia Lowndes and Mine Çetinkaya-Rundel, presented at RStudio Conference 2022. Illustrated by Allison Horst.](images/horst_quarto_schematic.png){fig-alt="A schematic representing the multi-language input (e.g. Python, R, Observable, Julia) and multi-format output (e.g. PDF, html, Word documents, and more) versatility of Quarto." fig-align="center"} ## A tour of Quarto

::: {.hand-purple-large style="text-align: center;"} Sit back and enjoy! ::: ## Your turn ::: task - Open [posit.cloud](https://posit.cloud) & login with your details - Open the workspace for the course: - Click "Start" next to the `exercises` project, wait for it to deploy - Open `hello-penguins.qmd` from the Files tab (bottom-right) - Render the document - Update the `author:` field with your name. Save the file. Re-render. - Change `format: html` to `format: typst` in the YAML header. Save, then re-render to produce a PDF. - Make one more change anywhere in the document, save, and re-render to see your edit appear. ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: ## From the comfort of your own workspace ::: r-stack ![](https://quarto.org/docs/tools/images/rstudio-preview.png){.fragment fig-alt="A screenshot of a Quarto document rendered inside RStudio" width="1200"} ![](https://quarto.org/docs/tools/images/jupyterlab-preview.png){.fragment fig-alt="A screenshot of a Quarto document rendered inside JupyterLab" width="1200"} ![](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/images/python-vscode.png){.fragment fig-alt="A screenshot of a Quarto document rendered inside VSCode" width="1200"} ::: # Quarto formats {background-color="#4C326A"} ## One install, "Batteries included" - RMarkdown grew into a large ecosystem, with **varying syntax**. . . . - Quarto comes **"batteries included"** straight out of the box - HTML reports and websites - PDF reports - MS Office (Word, Powerpoint) - Presentations (Powerpoint, Beamer, `revealjs`) - Books . . . - Any language, *exact* same approach and syntax ## One syntax, many formats ::: incremental - **Documents**: HTML, PDF, Word - **Presentations**: revealjs, beamer, pptx - **Multi-page**: websites, blogs, books - **Specialised**: journal articles, dashboards, interactive (Shiny) ::: . . . Any language: R, Python, Julia, Observable. Same syntax, same approach. ## Your turn {.small} ::: task In your exercises project in RStudio on Posit Cloud, go to [File \> New File \> Quarto document]{.highlight-yellow} to create a Quarto document with HTML output. Save the file as `my-first-document.qmd`, then render. Use the visual editor for the next steps. **Save (Cmd/Ctrl+S) before each render.** - In the YAML header at the top of the document, add a title in the `title:` field and your name to the `author:` field. - Create three sections in the document formatted as Heading 2, name these sections - Joyful tasks - Challenging tasks - A fact to learn - [**Stretch goal:**]{.highlight-yellow} In the YAML header add a table of contents in the `format:` field. Tip: check [quarto.org](https://quarto.org/) and use search {{< fa magnifying-glass >}} with "HTML table of contents". - [**Stretch goal:**]{.highlight-yellow} Change the html theme to `sketchy`. Tip: check [quarto.org](https://quarto.org/) and use search {{< fa magnifying-glass >}} with "HTML theming". - **Before you turn back**: without looking, what does the `toc:` field do? What is the difference between visual and source editor mode? ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: ## {.unlisted} ::: {.hand-purple style="text-align: center;"} Anything from Hello Quarto you'd like clarified before the break? ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: ## Take a break [Please get up and move!]{.highlight-yellow} Leave your emails alone. ![](images/pixel-art-tree.png){width="50%" fig-alt="This is the prompt generated by DALL-E 3 by OpenAI: A pixel art scene representing the concept of taking a break. The scene shows a serene outdoor setting with a clear blue sky. In the center, a small pixelated character is sitting comfortably on a bright green grassy hill, under the shade of a large, leafy tree. The character is depicted in a relaxed pose, perhaps sipping a warm beverage from a small cup. Nearby, a gentle stream flows, and a few fluffy white clouds drift in the sky. The overall mood of the image is peaceful and calming, emphasizing the tranquility of taking a break in nature."} ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html} ``` ::: ::: ::: footer Image generated with [DALL-E 3 by OpenAI](https://openai.com/blog/dall-e/) ::: ## Thanks! {{< fa sun >}} Slides created via revealjs and Quarto: All material is licensed under [Creative Commons Attribution Share Alike 4.0 International](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-sa/4.0/).

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
